  "weights": {

    "R1.7": 1.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": "",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.7": "The request is to check citations, so reference accuracy, sufficiency, and proper attribution are the load-bearing responsibilities.",
    "RX.1": "Any assessment must note uncertainty due to the limited excerpt and absence of a full reference list.",
    "RX.2": "The citation check should avoid asserting definitive errors or novelty judgments beyond what the given citations support.",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain scoped strictly to citation and scholarship aspects, not rewriting or methodological evaluation.",
    "RX.4": "Careful handling of citation issues helps prevent misattribution or reputational harm to authors or cited works.",
    "RX.5": "Findings should be tied to specific in-text mentions (e.g., Eyring et al., 2016; ACS; CMIP6/SSPs) to maintain traceability."
  }
}

rationale.R1.7: The request is to check citations, so reference accuracy, sufficiency, and proper attribution are the load-bearing responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: Any assessment must note uncertainty due to the limited excerpt and absence of a full reference list.
rationale.RX.2: The citation check should avoid asserting definitive errors or novelty judgments beyond what the given citations support.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain scoped strictly to citation and scholarship aspects, not rewriting or methodological evaluation.
rationale.RX.4: Careful handling of citation issues helps prevent misattribution or reputational harm to authors or cited works.
rationale.RX.5: Findings should be tied to specific in-text mentions (e.g., Eyring et al., 2016; ACS; CMIP6/SSPs) to maintain traceability.
